items.caption: A slide from a presentation, with a yellow background and black text. the title at the top reads "using the scorecard." below the title, there is a bulleted list outlining the features of a scorecard. the list includes points such as the scorecard adopting the same layout as a checklist for ease of use in assigning scores, the presentation of six broad pc obligations along with maximum points, and the breaking down of specific contract requirements into a number of result areas. the text also mentions that each results area requirement is allotted a maximum possible score for full compliance, and that points are scored individually for each requirement upon document or physical verification. the slide concludes by stating that scores for each key result area under the six obligations are summed up for the total score against which general pc compliance is assessed.
